NAME: SAGELD
FULL NAME: Scalable and Accurate Genome-wide G×E for Longitudinal Data
KEYWORDS: G×E, longitudinal data, saddlepoint approximation, GRM, matrix projection, repeated measures
ARROW_SUMMARY: Inputs: individual genotypes + longitudinal phenotypes + time-varying E + covariates (+ GRM) → matrix projection → SPA-adjusted score statistics → G×E interaction p-values
DESCRIPTION: A scalable G×E method for longitudinal traits that uses matrix projection for test statistics and the SPA GRM framework to control for sample relatedness. Achieves 10- to 10,000-fold speedups over existing methods while identifying 74 genetic×age and 5 genetic×adiposity loci in UK Biobank (407K individuals, 3.65M observations). Part of the GRAB R package (v0.2.0).
MANUAL_CITATION: Xu, H., Ma, Y., Liu, Y. et al. (2026). Leveraging longitudinal data to boost statistical power for gene–environment interaction analysis. Nature Computational Science. doi:10.1038/s43588-026-01002-z.
TITLE: Leveraging longitudinal data to boost statistical power for gene-environment interaction analysis.
Authors: Xu H,Ma Y,Liu Y,Li Y,Wan L,Zhang JF,Zhao Y,Yue W,Zhang P,Bi W
ABSTRACT: Gene-environment interaction (G×E) analyses play a crucial role in advancing genetic discovery, addressing missing heritability, and facilitating precision medicine. However, existing G×E methods are mostly designed for cross-sectional data, limiting the utility of longitudinal data. Here we propose SAGELD, a scalable and accurate genome-wide G×E method for longitudinal traits that controls for sample relatedness in large-scale datasets. SAGELD uses matrix projection to construct test statistics and the SPA GRM framework to efficiently control for sample relatedness, achieving 10- to 10,000-fold speedups over existing methods while maintaining greater power than cross-sectional analyses. We evaluated SAGELD through extensive simulations and UK Biobank analyses. Using age and body mass index as environmental exposures, we identified 74 loci with genetic × age interactions and 5 loci with genetic × adiposity interactions in the pooled analysis of longitudinal primary care data and cross-sectional assessment data. These results highlight the advantages of leveraging longitudinal data in G×E analyses.
CITATION: Xu H, Ma Y, Liu Y, Li Y, Wan L, Zhang JF, Zhao Y, Yue W, Zhang P, Bi W. (2026) Leveraging longitudinal data to boost statistical power for gene-environment interaction analysis. Nat Comput Sci. doi:10.1038/s43588-026-01002-z. PMID 42304093
